chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-2565 of 2016 C.P No.D-2190 of 2016 C.P No.D-2996 of 2018 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 11.02.2025 Mr. Bakhtiar Ahmed Panhwar, advocate for the petitioners Mr. Zulifiqar Ali Korai, advocate for respondent(s) Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri, Assistant A.G Sindh *** These petitions were filed seeking regularization; however, it is clearly mentioned in para-2 of the terms & conditions of the appointment orders of the petitioners that they will not have any right to claim for regularization or promotion and after accepting the said terms and conditions petitioners joined the service and presently they are no more in service, as such cannot claim regularization. Counsel for the petitioners has placed reliance on the Order dated 29.11.2023 passed by the Supreme Court in C.Ps No.1078-K to 1172-K of 2022. A careful perusal of said order reveals that it was passed with the consent of the parties rather on merit, as such no reliance can be made on the said order. In view of the above, these petitions are not maintainable and stand dismissed accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Sajjad Ali Jessar
